{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.3": 3, "R1.4": 2, "R1.7": 2, "RX.1": 5, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 3, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {"R1.3": "The output mentions contrastive disambiguation but doesn't explicitly link it to the evidence presented in the text.", "R1.4": "The output names a prior work (Patel and Rajagopal) but doesn't clearly articulate how DISAMBIG-RR differs from it.", "R1.7": "While the output mentions citations, it doesn't delve into the specifics of missing or incorrect citations.", "RX.1": "The output explicitly states uncertainty regarding the retrieval quality claim.", "RX.3": "The output claims to cover all requested dimensions but lacks concrete evidence to support this.", "RX.4": "The output does not identify any potential harmful content or regressions.", "RX.5": "The output doesn't provide details on how non-cosmetic changes are traced back to their sources."},
  "confidence": {"R1.3": 0.7, "R1.4": 0.6, "R1.7": 0.6, "RX.1": 0.9, "RX.3": 0.8, "RX.4": 0.7, "RX.5": 0.5},
  "blockers": []

rationale.R1.3: The output mentions contrastive disambiguation but doesn't explicitly link it to the evidence presented in the text.
rationale.R1.4: The output names a prior work (Patel and Rajagopal) but doesn't clearly articulate how DISAMBIG-RR differs from it.
rationale.R1.7: While the output mentions citations, it doesn't delve into the specifics of missing or incorrect citations.
rationale.RX.1: The output explicitly states uncertainty regarding the retrieval quality claim.
rationale.RX.3: The output claims to cover all requested dimensions but lacks concrete evidence to support this.
rationale.RX.4: The output does not identify any potential harmful content or regressions.
rationale.RX.5: The output doesn't provide details on how non-cosmetic changes are traced back to their sources.
